backtrace.c(3) Library Functions Manual backtrace.c(3)

NAME

backtrace.c - 📍 Backtrace capture, symbolization, and formatting implementation

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void backtrace_capture (backtrace_t *bt)
void backtrace_symbolize (backtrace_t *bt)
void backtrace_capture_and_symbolize (backtrace_t *bt)
void backtrace_t_free (backtrace_t *bt)
void backtrace_print_simple (int skip_frames)
void backtrace_print (const char *label, const backtrace_t *bt, int skip_frames, int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t filter)
void backtrace_print_many (const char *label, const backtrace_t *bts, int count)
int backtrace_format (char *buf, size_t buf_size, const char *label, const backtrace_t *bt, int skip_frames, int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t filter)

Detailed Description

📍 Backtrace capture, symbolization, and formatting implementation

Definition in file debug/backtrace.c.

Function Documentation

void backtrace_capture (backtrace_t * bt)

Definition at line 17 of file debug/backtrace.c.

17                                         {
18   if (!bt) {
19     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid backtrace");
20     return;
21   }
22   bt->count = platform_backtrace(bt->ptrs, 32);
23 }

void
  backtrace_capture_and_symbolize (backtrace_t * bt)


Definition at line 34 of file debug/backtrace.c.


34                                                       {
35   if (!bt) {
36     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid backtrace params");
37     return;
38   }

40   backtrace_symbolize(bt);

Referenced by asciichat_fatal_with_context().






int
  backtrace_format (char * buf, size_t buf_size, const char * label, const
  backtrace_t * bt, int skip_frames, int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t
  filter)


Definition at line 361 of file
  debug/backtrace.c.


362                                                                       {
363   if (!buf || buf_size == 0 || !bt || !bt->symbols || bt->count <= 0) {
364     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
365     return -1;
366   }
367 
368   // Capture count and symbols once to avoid TOCTOU race with other threads
369   int count = bt->count;
370   char **symbols = bt->symbols;
371 
372   int offset = 0;
373 
374   // Add label
375   offset += safe_snprintf(buf + offset, buf_size - (size_t)offset, "%s\n", label);
376 
377   // Calculate frame limits
378   int start = skip_frames;
379   int end = count;
380   if (max_frames > 0 && (start + max_frames) < end) {
381     end = start + max_frames;
382   }
383 
384   // Add frames
385   int frame_num = 0;
386   for (int i = start; i < end && i < count && offset < (int)buf_size - 128; i++) {
387     // Safely access symbols[i] - symbols array might be smaller than count or have NULL entries
388     const char *symbol = NULL;
389     if (symbols && symbols[i]) {
390       symbol = symbols[i];
391     } else {
392       symbol = "???";
393     }
394 
395     // Skip frame if filter says to
396     if (filter && filter(symbol)) {
397       continue;
398     }
399 
400     offset += safe_snprintf(buf + offset, buf_size - (size_t)offset, "  [%d] %s\n", frame_num, symbol);
401     frame_num++;
402   }
403 
404   return offset;
405 }






References safe_snprintf().






void
  backtrace_print (const char * label, const backtrace_t * bt, int skip_frames,
  int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t filter)


Definition at line 70 of file debug/backtrace.c.


71                                                       {
72   if (!bt || !bt->symbols || bt->count <= 0) {
73     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid backtrace params");
74     return;
75   }
76 
77   // Capture count and symbols once to avoid TOCTOU race with other threads
78   int count = bt->count;
79   char **symbols = bt->symbols;
80 
81   // Calculate frame limits
82   int start = skip_frames;
83   int end = count;
84   if (max_frames > 0 && (start + max_frames) < end) {
85     end = start + max_frames;
86   }
87 
88   // Build backtrace in two versions: colored for terminal, plain for log file
89   char colored_buffer[16384] = {0};
90   char plain_buffer[16384] = {0};
91   int colored_offset = 0;
92   int plain_offset = 0;
93 
94   // Format log header using logging system's template
95   char log_header_buf[512] = {0};
96   time_t now = time(NULL);
97   struct tm *tm_info = localtime(&now);
98   char timestamp[32];
99   strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%H:%M:%S", tm_info);
100 
101   thread_id_t tid = asciichat_thread_self();
102   uint64_t tid_val = (uintptr_t)tid;
103 
104   // Get current time in nanoseconds for template formatting
105   uint64_t time_ns = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() * 1000ULL;
106 
107   // Try to format header using the logging system's template with color
108   log_template_t *format = log_get_template();
109   if (format) {
110     // Color the label with WARN color for terminal output
111     const char *colored_label_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, label);
112     char colored_label_buf[256];
113     strncpy(colored_label_buf, colored_label_ptr, sizeof(colored_label_buf) - 1);
114     colored_label_buf[sizeof(colored_label_buf) - 1] = '\0';
115 
116     int len = log_template_apply(format, log_header_buf, sizeof(log_header_buf), LOG_WARN, timestamp, __FILE__,
117                                  __LINE__, __func__, tid_val, colored_label_buf, true, time_ns);
118     if (len > 0) {
119       // Successfully formatted with logging template
120       colored_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_buffer + colored_offset, sizeof(colored_buffer) - (size_t)colored_offset,
121                                       "%s\n", log_header_buf);
122     } else {
123       // Fallback: manual formatting if template fails
124       safe_snprintf(log_header_buf, sizeof(log_header_buf), "[%s] [WARN] [tid:%llu] %s: %s", timestamp, tid_val,
125                     __func__, label);
126       const char *colored_header_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, log_header_buf);
127       char colored_header_buf[512];
128       strncpy(colored_header_buf, colored_header_ptr, sizeof(colored_header_buf) - 1);
129       colored_header_buf[sizeof(colored_header_buf) - 1] = '\0';
130       colored_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_buffer + colored_offset, sizeof(colored_buffer) - (size_t)colored_offset,
131                                       "%s\n", colored_header_buf);
132     }
133   } else {
134     // Fallback: manual formatting if no template available
135     safe_snprintf(log_header_buf, sizeof(log_header_buf), "[%s] [WARN] [tid:%llu] %s: %s", timestamp, tid_val, __func__,
136                   label);
137     const char *colored_header_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, log_header_buf);
138     char colored_header_buf[512];
139     strncpy(colored_header_buf, colored_header_ptr, sizeof(colored_header_buf) - 1);
140     colored_header_buf[sizeof(colored_header_buf) - 1] = '\0';
141     colored_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_buffer + colored_offset, sizeof(colored_buffer) - (size_t)colored_offset,
142                                     "%s\n", colored_header_buf);
143   }
144 
145   // Add plain label header for log file
146   plain_offset +=
147       safe_snprintf(plain_buffer + plain_offset, sizeof(plain_buffer) - (size_t)plain_offset, "%s\n", label);
148 
149   // Build backtrace frames with colored output for terminal, plain for log
150   int frame_num = 0;
151   for (int i = start; i < end && i < count && colored_offset < (int)sizeof(colored_buffer) - 512; i++) {
152     // Safely access symbols[i] - symbols array might be smaller than count or have NULL entries
153     const char *symbol = NULL;
154     if (symbols && symbols[i]) {
155       symbol = symbols[i];
156     } else {
157       symbol = "???";
158     }
159 
160     // Skip frame if filter says to
161     if (filter && filter(symbol)) {
162       continue;
163     }
164 
165     // Build frame number string
166     char frame_num_str[16];
167     safe_snprintf(frame_num_str, sizeof(frame_num_str), "%d", frame_num);
168 
169     // Get colored frame number - copy to temp buffer to avoid rotating buffer issues
170     const char *colored_num_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, frame_num_str);
171     char colored_num_buf[256];
172     strncpy(colored_num_buf, colored_num_ptr, sizeof(colored_num_buf) - 1);
173     colored_num_buf[sizeof(colored_num_buf) - 1] = '\0';
174 
175     // Parse symbol to extract parts for selective coloring
176     // Format: "[binary_name] function_name() (file:line)"
177     char colored_symbol[2048] = {0};
178     const char *s = symbol;
179     int colored_sym_offset = 0;
180 
181     // Color binary name between brackets
182     if (*s == '[') {
183       colored_sym_offset +=
184           safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "[");
185       s++;
186       // Find closing bracket
187       const char *bracket_end = strchr(s, ']');
188       if (bracket_end) {
189         int bin_len = bracket_end - s;
190         char bin_name[512];
191         strncpy(bin_name, s, bin_len);
192         bin_name[bin_len] = '\0';
193         const char *colored_bin_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, bin_name);
194         char colored_bin_buf[512];
195         strncpy(colored_bin_buf, colored_bin_ptr, sizeof(colored_bin_buf) - 1);
196         colored_bin_buf[sizeof(colored_bin_buf) - 1] = '\0';
197         colored_sym_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset,
198                                             sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "%s", colored_bin_buf);
199         colored_sym_offset +=
200             safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "] ");
201         s = bracket_end + 1;
202       }
203     }
204 
205     // Skip leading spaces after bracket
206     while (*s && *s == ' ')
207       s++;
208 
209     // Parse: could be "function() (file:line)" or "file:line (unresolved)"
210     const char *paren_start = strchr(s, '(');
211 
212     // Detect format: if there's a colon before the first paren, it's "file:line (description)"
213     const char *colon_pos = strchr(s, ':');
214     if (colon_pos && paren_start && colon_pos < paren_start) {
215       // Format: "file:line (unresolved function)" - rearrange to "(unresolved) (file:line)"
216       // Extract file:line part (trim trailing spaces)
217       int file_len = paren_start - s;
218       while (file_len > 0 && s[file_len - 1] == ' ')
219         file_len--;
220       char file_part[512];
221       strncpy(file_part, s, file_len);
222       file_part[file_len] = '\0';
223 
224       // Extract description content (without parens)
225       const char *paren_end = strchr(paren_start, ')');
226       int desc_content_len = paren_end - paren_start - 1; // -1 to skip opening paren
227       char desc_content[512];
228       strncpy(desc_content, paren_start + 1, desc_content_len); // +1 to skip opening paren
229       desc_content[desc_content_len] = '\0';
230 
231       const char *colored_desc_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, desc_content);
232       char colored_desc_buf[512];
233       strncpy(colored_desc_buf, colored_desc_ptr, sizeof(colored_desc_buf) - 1);
234       colored_desc_buf[sizeof(colored_desc_buf) - 1] = '\0';
235       colored_sym_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset,
236                                           sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "(%s)", colored_desc_buf);
237 
238       // Now color file:line in parens
239       // Skip leading spaces in file_part
240       const char *file_start = file_part;
241       while (*file_start && *file_start == ' ')
242         file_start++;
243 
244       char *file_colon = strchr(file_start, ':');
245       if (file_colon) {
246         int filename_len = file_colon - file_start;
247         char filename[512];
248         strncpy(filename, file_start, filename_len);
249         filename[filename_len] = '\0';
250 
251         const char *colored_file_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, filename);
252         char colored_file_buf[512];
253         strncpy(colored_file_buf, colored_file_ptr, sizeof(colored_file_buf) - 1);
254         colored_file_buf[sizeof(colored_file_buf) - 1] = '\0';
255 
256         const char *line_num = file_colon + 1;
257         const char *colored_line_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, line_num);
258         char colored_line_buf[512];
259         strncpy(colored_line_buf, colored_line_ptr, sizeof(colored_line_buf) - 1);
260         colored_line_buf[sizeof(colored_line_buf) - 1] = '\0';
261 
262         colored_sym_offset +=
263             safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, " (%s:%s)",
264                           colored_file_buf, colored_line_buf);
265       }
266     } else if (paren_start) {
267       // Format: "function() (file:line)"
268       int func_len = paren_start - s;
269       char func_name[512];
270       strncpy(func_name, s, func_len);
271       func_name[func_len] = '\0';
272 
273       const char *colored_func_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEV, func_name);
274       char colored_func_buf[512];
275       strncpy(colored_func_buf, colored_func_ptr, sizeof(colored_func_buf) - 1);
276       colored_func_buf[sizeof(colored_func_buf) - 1] = '\0';
277       colored_sym_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset,
278                                           sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "%s()", colored_func_buf);
279 
280       // Find file:line in second set of parens
281       s = paren_start + 1;
282       while (*s && *s != '(')
283         s++;
284 
285       if (*s == '(') {
286         const char *file_paren_end = strchr(s, ')');
287         if (file_paren_end) {
288           colored_sym_offset +=
289               safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, " (");
290           int file_len = file_paren_end - s - 1;
291           char file_part[512];
292           strncpy(file_part, s + 1, file_len);
293           file_part[file_len] = '\0';
294 
295           // Skip leading spaces in file_part
296           const char *file_start = file_part;
297           while (*file_start && *file_start == ' ')
298             file_start++;
299 
300           char *colon_pos = strchr(file_start, ':');
301           if (colon_pos) {
302             int filename_len = colon_pos - file_start;
303             char filename[512];
304             strncpy(filename, file_start, filename_len);
305             filename[filename_len] = '\0';
306 
307             const char *colored_file_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, filename);
308             char colored_file_buf[512];
309             strncpy(colored_file_buf, colored_file_ptr, sizeof(colored_file_buf) - 1);
310             colored_file_buf[sizeof(colored_file_buf) - 1] = '\0';
311 
312             const char *line_num = colon_pos + 1;
313             const char *colored_line_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, line_num);
314             char colored_line_buf[512];
315             strncpy(colored_line_buf, colored_line_ptr, sizeof(colored_line_buf) - 1);
316             colored_line_buf[sizeof(colored_line_buf) - 1] = '\0';
317 
318             colored_sym_offset +=
319                 safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "%s:%s",
320                               colored_file_buf, colored_line_buf);
321           }
322           colored_sym_offset +=
323               safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, ")");
324         }
325       }
326     } else {
327       // No parens, likely a hex address - color with FATAL
328       const char *colored_addr_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, s);
329       char colored_addr_buf[512];
330       strncpy(colored_addr_buf, colored_addr_ptr, sizeof(colored_addr_buf) - 1);
331       colored_addr_buf[sizeof(colored_addr_buf) - 1] = '\0';
332       colored_sym_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset,
333                                           sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "%s", colored_addr_buf);
334     }
335 
336     // Format colored buffer: "  [colored_num] colored_symbol\n"
337     colored_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_buffer + colored_offset, sizeof(colored_buffer) - (size_t)colored_offset,
338                                     "  [%s] %s\n", colored_num_buf, colored_symbol);
339 
340     // Format plain buffer: "  [num] symbol\n"
341     plain_offset += safe_snprintf(plain_buffer + plain_offset, sizeof(plain_buffer) - (size_t)plain_offset,
342                                   "  [%d] %s\n", frame_num, symbol);
343     frame_num++;
344   }
345 
346   // Output backtrace through logging system (no direct fprintf to avoid ANSI codes in captured output)
347   log_warn("%s", plain_buffer);
348   log_file_msg("%s", plain_buffer);
349 }






References asciichat_thread_self(),
    colored_string(), log_file_msg(), log_get_template(),
    log_template_apply(), platform_get_monotonic_time_us(), and
    safe_snprintf().

void
  backtrace_print_many (const char * label, const backtrace_t * bts, int
  count)


Definition at line 351 of file
  debug/backtrace.c.


351                                                                                 {
352   if (!bts || count <= 0) {
353     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: bts is NULL or count <= 0");
354     return;
355   }
356   for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
357     backtrace_print(label, &bts[i], 0, 0, NULL);
358   }
359 }






References backtrace_print().






void
  backtrace_print_simple (int skip_frames)


Definition at line 54 of file debug/backtrace.c.


54                                              {
55   void *buffer[32];
56   int size = platform_backtrace(buffer, 32);
57 
58   if (size > 0) {
59     char **symbols = platform_backtrace_symbols(buffer, size);
60 
61     // Skip platform_print_backtrace itself (1 frame) + any additional frames requested
62     backtrace_print_symbols("Backtrace", symbols, size, 1 + skip_frames, 0, NULL);
63 
64     platform_backtrace_symbols_destroy(symbols);
65   } else {
66     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to capture backtrace");
67   }
68 }






References platform_backtrace(),
    platform_backtrace_symbols(), and
    platform_backtrace_symbols_destroy().


Referenced by main().






void
  backtrace_symbolize (backtrace_t * bt)


Definition at line 25 of file debug/backtrace.c.


25                                           {
26   if (!bt || bt->tried_symbolize) {
27     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid symbolize params");
28     return; // Already tried or invalid (don't try twice)
29   }
30   bt->tried_symbolize = true;
31   bt->symbols = platform_backtrace_symbols(bt->ptrs, bt->count);
32 }

void
  backtrace_t_free (backtrace_t * bt)


Definition at line 43 of file debug/backtrace.c.


43                                        {
44   if (!bt) {
45     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid backtrace params");
46     return;
47   }
48   if (bt->symbols) {
49     platform_backtrace_symbols_destroy(bt->symbols);
50     bt->symbols = NULL;
51   }
52 }
